# Roadmapped

> Project management as flat files in your repo. Backlog, roadmap and docs live
> as plain YAML and markdown inside your repository — the only source of truth.
> Driven by your AI agent through a CLI and a Claude skill. No database, no SaaS,
> no account. MIT licensed, free.

Roadmapped turns a git repository into its own project management tool. Tasks are
YAML files under `docs/tasks/`. The roadmap (done / available / locked states) is
computed from the dependency graph on every read, never stored. The dashboard and
the CLI read and write the same data through the same validator; on error the
change rolls back and ids are never reused. Git history is the audit log.

It is agent-first: a CLI (`npx roadmapped`) and a Claude skill let an AI agent
create specs, tasks and dependencies in the correct schema, and record what it
ships (outcome, verification, commit). A git hook refuses commits not attached to
a task. Roadmapped's own backlog is managed by Roadmapped — the repository is the
proof.

## Key facts

- Data model: flat YAML/markdown files in the user's repo. No database, no server.
- 8 fixed stages: Idea, Initial, Identity, Build, GTM, Launch, Scale, Mature.
- Interfaces: React dashboard (Vite), a CLI, and a Claude skill (also an MCP server).
- License: MIT. Author: Rémi Courtillon.
- Privacy: data never leaves the user's machine; "delete account" is `rm -rf`.

## Links

- Home: https://roadmapped.work/
- Source: https://github.com/remicourtillon/Roadmapped
- Guide: https://github.com/remicourtillon/Roadmapped/blob/main/docs/guide.md
- Public backlog (dogfooding proof): https://github.com/remicourtillon/Roadmapped/tree/main/docs/tasks
